core_question: Can a 150-instance stratified gold-label benchmark with single-annotator IAA produce defensible P/R/F1 evidence for the four-task pipeline upgrades, without contaminating measurement with the very components it is meant to evaluate?
knowns: 8 accepted edges currently exist in artifacts/microbe_feature_relations.jsonl; one (Edge #5) is a known entity-type error.
_extract_candidates() in scripts/extract_microbe_feature_relations.py produces 13 substring-vocab candidates against the 1,016-paper corpus.

Gemini self-consistency rejection records are NOT persisted to disk today — only accepted edges are written, so gemini_rejected stratum can be recovered as (candidates − accepted) but per-sample vote counts are lost.
BioClinical-ModernBERT-base loads cleanly on conda base; faiss-cpu is not yet pinned in dependency tracking.
TUI accept set was {T007, T194, T204} as of last session; user has now approved adding T005 for virome future-proofing.
236 tests pass on the full suite post-Task-3 implementation; no regressions.
unknowns: True precision / recall of the 8 currently-accepted edges against ground truth — the entire reason the gold set exists; cannot be measured before the gold set is hand-labeled.
What fraction of vocab-excluded sentences mention real body-composition features (recall ceiling of substring matching). The vocab_excluded stratum probes this but only the user's labels resolve it.
Whether intra-annotator Cohen's κ via 2-week temporal re-labeling will meet the κ ≥ 0.80 floor — schema clarity directly determines this and cannot be tested before the user labels.
How many of the 13 substring-found candidates Gemini rejected are actually associated (false negatives of the relation classifier) versus correctly rejected. The gemini_rejected stratum probes this.
Whether the binary collapse of {associated_positive, associated_negative, associated_unsigned} into pred=1 versus a 3-class signed evaluation will hide systematic direction errors in the pipeline.
assumptions: A 150-instance gold set is large enough to estimate P/R/F1 with ±0.07 confidence intervals at the binary-class level — based on the standard normal approximation for binomial proportions at p≈0.7, n=150, σ ≈ √(0.21/150) ≈ 0.037, 95% CI ≈ ±0.07.

Stratification (15 accepted / 50 gemini-rejected / 35 vocab-excluded / 30 recall-probe / 20 random) gives separately-estimable precision and recall; balanced random sampling would waste budget on uninformative easy cases at the current corpus's sparse-positive distribution.

Treating no_association_explicit (null-finding citations) as gold=0 in the binary scorer is correct given the current pipeline's accept policy — the pipeline does not emit null-finding edges, so a null-finding sentence should NOT be predicted as associated. Multi-class scoring is future work.

Reusing scripts/extract_microbe_feature_relations.py:_extract_candidates as the candidate generator preserves identity with the production pipeline. If we re-implemented sampling we'd risk evaluating against a different candidate space than what the pipeline actually sees.
disconfirmation: After hand-labeling the 150-instance gold set, if measured precision on the accepted stratum drops below 0.50 (i.e., more than half of the 8 currently-accepted edges are wrong on independent inspection), the entire pipeline acceptance policy needs redesign — Gemini 7/7 self-consistency is then demonstrably insufficient and the four-task upgrade is itself measuring on a contaminated baseline. Conversely, if intra-annotator κ falls below 0.60 on temporal re-labeling, the annotation schema itself is the primary problem, not the pipeline.
